content.intro: References are the core of PropertyGoose. When you create a reference, the tenant receives an email with a link to complete their reference questionnaire. The system then collects employment references, landlord references, credit checks, and ID verification.

steps.title: Navigate to References
steps.content: Click 'References' in the sidebar navigation to open the References page.

steps.title: Click 'Create New Reference'
steps.content: Click the 'Create New Reference' button to open the reference creation modal.

steps.title: Select number of tenants
steps.content: Use the 'Number of Tenants' selector to specify how many tenants will be on this tenancy (1-15). Each tenant will receive their own reference questionnaire.

steps.title: Choose property entry mode
steps.content: Toggle between 'Select from Properties' to choose an existing property from your portfolio, or 'Enter Manually' to type in a new property address.

steps.title: Enter property details
steps.content: If entering manually, fill in the property address, city, and postcode. Add the monthly rent amount and proposed move-in date.

steps.title: Add tenant details
steps.content: For each tenant, enter their title, first name, last name, email address, phone number, and date of birth.

steps.title: Add guarantors (optional)
steps.content: If any tenant requires a guarantor, tick the guarantor checkbox and enter the guarantor's details. They'll receive a separate reference form.

steps.title: Submit the reference
steps.content: Review all details and click 'Create Reference'. All tenants and guarantors will receive email invitations to complete their references.

sections.title: Import from Offer
sections.content: If you've already sent an offer to this tenant, you can import their details when creating the reference to avoid re-entering information.

sections.title: Lead Tenant
sections.content: The first tenant you enter is automatically designated as the 'lead tenant'. This person will be the primary contact for communications about the tenancy.
